g.text: FAVOR CARE — Information Architecture & Page Templates
Restructured website journey · Donation paths · Storytelling · Volunteer flows
Benchmarked against Children's Society, Farm Africa, WaterAid, Obama Foundation, SolarAid & Eden Reforestation Projects
Page templates → see annotated cards in lower section
All shapes are individual SVG elements — text, fills and connectors stay editable when imported into Miro.
Logo · Mega-menu (5 items) · Search · Prayer Hub · DONATE button (sticky, high-contrast) · Language/Region toggle
Hero video · Mission statement · Impact stats
Who we are, what we believe, why it matters
Programs organized by pillar of impact
UTILITY & FOOTER PAGES (accessible globally from footer)
PAGE TEMPLATES — Wireframe specs for the 6 most critical pages
inspired by Obama Foundation + WaterAid + Farm Africa
Mission · Programs · Stories · Get Involved · Donate
"Giving hope for a better future to those in need."
IMPACT STATS — animated count-up on scroll
Two-column: short narrative + portrait of beneficiary
(Farm Africa scroll-zoom on a single keyword)
Build a school that can hold 500 kids
Below this fold: Story spotlight (Obama-style split scroll) → Prayer Hub join → Recent news → Newsletter sign-up → Footer
inspired by SolarAid + Children's Society + WaterAid
Donate / Choose how you want to give
Your gift, in their hands within days.
Pick the path that fits you. Every option is secure, multi-currency, and tax-receipted.
Direct line to our development team
WHY TRUST FAVOR CARE WITH YOUR GIFT?
✓ Audited annual financials · ✓ SEC-registered foundation · ✓ 90%+ of every gift goes direct to programs
✓ Tax-deductible receipts (PH, US, AU) · ✓ Real photos & impact reports for every donor · ✓ Faith-led, community-rooted
Critical UX: every donation amount is contextualised with what it does — Children's Society principle. Multi-currency built-in.
TEMPLATE C · PROGRAM PAGE (e.g. Redemptio School)
Free Christian education for kids on the Taytay floodway. We believe education breaks poverty.
Short narrative — who, where, what's at stake.
3 short paragraphs · what we actually do day-to-day.
"Before Redemptio, I never thought I'd learn to read."
— Maria, Grade 2 · Her mom is a sari-sari store helper. Tap to read her full story.
Fund a student's books, uniform, or meals.
Tutor, mentor, or run an event at the school.
Sponsor a classroom or fund the new wing.
Same template applies to Prison Ministry · Health · Disaster Relief · Community Dev. Filter system across program pages (Farm Africa).
inspired by Obama Foundation split-scroll + WaterAid
"I want to be a nurse one day."
How Maria, age 8, went from missing school to leading her class.
Published Nov 2025 · 5 min read · Photos by [photographer]
LEFT PANEL · sticky narrative (anchors as user scrolls)
Single column · 60–70 char width · serif body font.
Sub-headings break the read into 3–4 chapters:
My teacher gave me a book about
a girl who became a doctor."
Maria's story can be another child's story. Sponsor a Redemptio student today.
Every story page ends with a contextual CTA tied to the program in the story. Related stories carousel below the fold.
inspired by SolarAid + Surfers Against Sewage
There's a place for you here.
Pick how you want to show up — every gift of time, skill or prayer matters.
Monthly stories, prayer points, and ways to act. Unsubscribe anytime.
Every card has its own application/intake form. Live volunteer count + most-needed roles surfaced dynamically.
